problems.question:
そのバスに乗り遅れると30分待つことになる。
(for / the bus / missing / half / waiting / means) an hour.
problems.rawAnswer: Missing the bus means waiting for half
problems.ja: そのバスに乗り遅れると30分待つことになる。
problems.answer: Missing the bus means waiting for half
problems.en: Missing the bus means waiting for half an hour.

problems.question: He is fond of (　　) soccer.
problems.ja: 彼はサッカーをするのが好きだ。
problems.en: He is fond of playing soccer.

problems.question: When I had dinner with Tom, I got angry at (　　) on his cell phone throughout the meal.
problems.ja: トムと食事をしたとき，その間ずっと彼が携帯電話で話していたことに私は腹を立てた。
problems.en: When I had dinner with Tom, I got angry at his talking on his cell phone throughout the meal.

problems.question:
彼女の表情から，彼女は子ども扱いされるのが嫌いなのだとわかる。
Her expression shows that she (child / like / treated / dislikes / being / a).
problems.rawAnswer: dislikes being treated like a child
problems.ja: 彼女の表情から，彼女は子ども扱いされるのが嫌いなのだとわかる。
problems.answer: dislikes being treated like a child
problems.en: Her expression shows that she dislikes being treated like a child.

problems.question:
彼は20年前に有名な俳優だったことを誇りにしている。
(been / proud / 20 years ago / having / of / is / he / a well-known actor).
problems.rawAnswer: He is proud of having been a well-known actor 20 years ago
problems.ja: 彼は20年前に有名な俳優だったことを誇りにしている。
problems.answer: He is proud of having been a well-known actor 20 years ago
problems.en: He is proud of having been a well-known actor 20 years ago.

problems.question: I am ashamed (　　) kind to the old woman on the train.
problems.ja: 電車の中で，老婦人に親切にしなかったことを私は恥じている。
problems.en: I am ashamed of not having been kind to the old woman on the train.

problems.question:
彼女にそれをするなと言ってもむだである。（１語不要）
It is (do / to / use / no / telling / useless / not / her) it.
problems.rawAnswer: no use telling her not to do（useless不要）
problems.ja: 彼女にそれをするなと言ってもむだである。（１語不要）
problems.answer: no use telling her not to do
problems.en: It is no use telling her not to do it.

problems.question: The line is dead. There is no use (　　) to use the phone.
problems.ja: 回線が切れている。電話を使おうとしてもむだだよ。
problems.en: The line is dead. There is no use trying to use the phone.

problems.question: There is no point (　　) an expensive personal computer if you never use it.
problems.ja: 使うことがまったくないのなら，高価なパソコンを持っていてもむだである。
problems.en: There is no point in having an expensive personal computer if you never use it.

problems.question: I ①<u>look</u> forward ②<u>to see</u> you ③<u>in</u> Japan ④<u>sometime</u> soon.
problems.ja: 近いうちに日本であなたに会えることを楽しみにしています。
problems.en: I look forward to seeing you in Japan sometime soon.

problems.question: She is not used to (　　) formal letters.
problems.ja: 彼女は正式な手紙を書くことに慣れていない。
problems.en: She is not used to writing formal letters.

problems.question:
私は人前で話すのには不慣れです。（１語不要）
I'm (in / not / public / speaking / to / accustomed / speak).
problems.rawAnswer: not accustomed to speaking in public（speak不要）
problems.ja: 私は人前で話すのには不慣れです。（１語不要）
problems.answer: not accustomed to speaking in public
problems.en: I'm not accustomed to speaking in public.

problems.question: She did not object (　　) late at night.
problems.ja: 彼女は，私が夜遅くまで働くことに反対しなかった。
problems.en: She did not object to my working late at night.

problems.question: When it comes (　　), John is definitely the best in class.
problems.ja: 走ることとなると，ジョンは間違いなくクラスで一番だ。
problems.en: When it comes to running, John is definitely the best in class.

problems.question: What do you say (　　) tennis on Sunday?
problems.ja: 日曜日にテニスをしませんか。
problems.en: What do you say to playing tennis on Sunday?

problems.question:
彼は学生の質問すべてに答えるのに苦労した。
He (all / answering / difficulty / had / questions / the) asked by the students.
problems.rawAnswer: had difficulty answering all the questions
problems.ja: 彼は学生の質問すべてに答えるのに苦労した。
problems.answer: had difficulty answering all the questions
problems.en: He had difficulty answering all the questions asked by the students.

problems.question: People often have great (　　) my handwriting.
problems.ja: 人々は私の手書きの字を読むのにしばしば四苦八苦している。
problems.en: People often have great trouble reading my handwriting.

problems.question: They are very busy (　　) their homework, aren't they?
problems.ja: 彼らは宿題を片付けるのに大忙しですね。
problems.en: They are very busy doing their homework, aren't they?

problems.question:
私はいつも宿題をするのに２時間を費やす。
I usually (hours / my / doing / spend / two / homework).
problems.rawAnswer: spend two hours doing my homework
problems.ja: 私はいつも宿題をするのに２時間を費やす。
problems.answer: spend two hours doing my homework
problems.en: I usually spend two hours doing my homework.

problems.question: There is (　　) when John will show up.
problems.ja: いつジョンが現れるかわからない（←いつジョンが現れるかを判断することはできない）。
problems.en: There is no telling when John will show up.

problems.question:
喫煙が健康に悪いことは言うまでもない。
It (for / smoking / saying / goes / bad / without / is / that) one's health.
problems.rawAnswer: goes without saying that smoking is bad for
problems.ja: 喫煙が健康に悪いことは言うまでもない。
problems.answer: goes without saying that smoking is bad for
problems.en: It goes without saying that smoking is bad for one's health.

problems.question: She didn't mean to make him feel like (　　).
problems.ja: 彼女は，彼を泣きたい気分にさせるつもりはなかった。
problems.en: She didn't mean to make him feel like crying.

problems.question: (　　), she smiled quietly to herself.
problems.ja: そのニュースを聞くとすぐに，彼女はひとり静かに笑った。
problems.en: On hearing the news, she smiled quietly to herself.

problems.question: (　　) trying to make a decision, it is important to keep a proper sense of priority.
problems.ja: 決断をしようとするときは，何を優先すべきかの正しい感覚を保つことが重要だ。
problems.en: In trying to make a decision, it is important to keep a proper sense of priority.
